introduction: Logarithms are the inverse of exponentiation: if a^x = N, then log_a(N) = x. Exam questions test product/quotient/power rules, change of base, comparing logs, and solving equations by converting between exponential and logarithmic form.
explanation:
CONCEPT EXPLANATION

For base a > 0, a ≠ 1: log_a(N) is defined only when N > 0. Fundamental identity: a^(log_a N) = N and log_a(a^x) = x.

Key properties: log(MN) = log M + log N; log(M/N) = log M − log N; log(M^k) = k log M. Change of base: log_a b = (log_c b)/(log_c a) for any valid base c. Common bases: log_10 (common log) and log_e = ln (natural log).

REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS

Logarithmic scales measure earthquake magnitude (Richter), sound intensity (decibels), pH in chemistry, and exponential growth/decay in finance and biology.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Applying log(a + b) = log a + log b (false).
2) Forgetting domain: argument of log must be positive.
3) Dropping base when equating logarithms — bases must match.
4) Sign errors when log(M/N) = log M − log N.
5) Misusing change of base by inverting numerator and denominator.

IMPORTANT NOTES

• log_a 1 = 0; log_a a = 1; log_a (1/a) = −1.
• If log_a x = log_a y then x = y (same base).
• Compare logs by comparing arguments only when base > 1 and both arguments positive.
• log without subscript usually means base 10.
• ln means base e ≈ 2.718.

formulas.title: Definition
formulas.content: If a^x = N (a > 0, a ≠ 1, N > 0), then x = log_a(N)

formulas.title: Change of base
formulas.content: log_a b = (log_c b) / (log_c a) = (ln b) / (ln a)

formulas.title: Exponential–logarithmic inverse
formulas.content: a^(log_a N) = N and log_a(a^x) = x

shortcutTips.title: Same base equality
shortcutTips.tip: If log_a x = log_a y, then x = y directly — no need to expand.

shortcutTips.title: Convert to single log
shortcutTips.tip: Combine sums and differences into one log before equating to a constant.

shortcutTips.title: Change of base choice
shortcutTips.tip: Use ln or log_10 for change of base — pick whichever appears in the question.

shortcutTips.title: Compare arguments
shortcutTips.tip: For base > 1, larger argument means larger log value.

shortcutTips.title: Watch the domain
shortcutTips.tip: Reject solutions that make any log argument zero or negative.

solvedExamples.problem: Express log_8 64 in terms of log_2.
